news.title: Private equity investments in real estate rise to $4.3 billion in FY26: Report
news.para: Private equity investments in India's real estate sector reached $4.3 billion in FY26. This marks a 16% rise from FY25 and the highest deal activity in seven years. Commercial office assets attracted the most investment. Domestic capital inflows also saw a significant increase. The National Capital Region led deal activity among cities.

news.title: Harmandeep Singh Kandhari led Harmony Infra Ventures: Building real estate with structure, scale, and delivery discipline
news.para: Harmony Infra Ventures is emerging as a new real estate developer in India. Led by Harmandeep Singh Kandhari, the company focuses on structured planning and timely project delivery. They aim for long-term value creation and transparency with buyers. Harmony Infra Ventures prioritizes disciplined execution and commercial viability in its developments.

news.title: Bidhannagar civic body's online property tax collection to start on April 13
news.para: Bidhannagar Municipal Corporation is launching online property tax collection for the 2026-27 financial year. Residents of Salt Lake can pay online from Monday after receiving demand notices. Rajarhat Gopalpur residents can make offline payments at designated branches starting April 16. This initiative aims to streamline tax collection for both areas.

news.title: About 40% property owners in Belagavi miss tax payments
news.para: Nearly 40% of property owners in Belagavi failed to pay their property tax dues for the 2025-26 financial year, leaving the Belagavi City Corporation with a significant revenue gap. Despite a target of Rs 78 crore plus arrears, only Rs 70.8 crore was collected, impacting development works and raising questions about administrative efficiency.

news.title: Kanpur: 20 held for duping applicants through fake PMAY scheme
news.para: Kanpur police have busted a Jamtara-style cybercrime network in Reuna village, arresting 20 individuals for duping Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana applicants. The gang used fake government schemes, posing as officials to extract Aadhaar details and then demanding registration fees via digital payment apps. Some members also impersonated police officers to extort money from victims.

news.title: Stamp duty, registration revenue up 30% at ₹19,000 crore in Gujarat
news.para: Gujarat's stamp duty and registration revenue surged 30% to Rs 19,102.58 crore in 2025-26, driven by a modest increase in property registrations and higher property values, partly due to doubled jantri rates. Despite strong overall demand, new project registrations have seen a decline.

news.title: GQG Partners sells shares worth ₹197 crore in ITC Hotels through bulk deal
news.para: Foreign investor GQG Partners divested ITC Hotels shares valued at Rs 197 crore. This occurred as the stock experienced a significant decline. Despite the sale, analysts maintain a positive long-term outlook. Growth is anticipated from new hotel properties and real estate ventures. Brokerages suggest the recent stock drop is excessive and a rebound is possible.

news.title: Auction of 1,400 shops in Telangana's 2BHK colonies begins on April 9
news.para: Over 1,400 shops in Telangana's 2BHK housing colonies are being auctioned to boost local economies and improve colony upkeep. The sales, starting April 9th, aim to provide employment and essential goods, with revenue dedicated to colony maintenance managed by resident welfare associations.

news.title: RWAs in Delhi sound alarm on mix-use development
news.para: Resident Welfare Associations are raising alarms about the new Transit-Oriented Development policy. This policy permits mixed-use development near metro and railway stations. Residents fear this will lead to increased traffic and pollution. They cite past experiences with similar policies that have already made residential areas chaotic.

news.title: After 15-year wait, JAL homebuyers pin hopes on Adani plan for long-delayed homes
news.para: Homebuyers await possession of Jaiprakash Associates Limited properties after 15 years. The Supreme Court's decision not to halt Adani Group's Rs 14,500 crore resolution plan offers renewed hope.  Adani Group's plan aims to deliver homes within two years or offer refunds.  This development is crucial for thousands of buyers facing long delays.

news.title: Signature Global's pre-sales fall 20% to ₹8,220 crore in FY26
news.para: Signature Global India saw pre-sales drop 20% to ₹8,220 crore in FY2026.  Despite lower unit sales, average prices rose.  The company significantly cut its net debt to a historic low of ₹200 crore.  A new joint venture marks its entry into commercial real estate development in the NCR region.

news.title: Builders in UK report biggest leap in cost inflation on record: Survey
news.para: British construction firms experienced record one-month cost inflation in March, with input costs soaring. New orders declined at the fastest pace since November, and future output expectations weakened. Disruption in the Strait of Hormuz also worsened supply chain performance, impacting the sector's challenging near-term outlook.

news.title: Union cabinet approves Jaipur metro project phase-II costing over ₹13,000 crore
news.para: The Union Cabinet has approved the Jaipur Metro Phase-2, a 41-km corridor with 36 stations, costing over Rs 13,000 crore. This North-South corridor will enhance connectivity to key areas, integrate with Phase-1, and is targeted for completion by September 2031, aiming to reduce congestion and improve urban mobility.

news.title: China Vanke offers 40% upfront payment in return for bond extension: Sources
news.para: China Vanke is offering to repay 40% of a yuan bond principal upfront for a one-year extension, seeking to ease "particularly acute" repayment pressure. This follows a similar successful extension for three other bonds in January. The developer faces significant bond maturities between April and July, amidst a broader property sector crisis.

news.title: Proptimes closes FY26 with over ₹61 crore revenue, sets ₹100 crore target for FY27
news.para: Proptimes Consultancy Services Pvt. Ltd. has crossed a significant revenue milestone of over Rs 61 crore for FY 2025-26. The real estate consulting firm is now aiming for over Rs 100 crore in revenue for FY 2026-27. Proptimes has expanded its operations to the UAE. The company is focused on growth through technology and partnerships with developers.

news.title: Ashiana Housing pre-sales jump over two-fold to ₹1,290 crore in Q4 FY26
news.para: Ashiana Housing has reported a remarkable surge in sales bookings, more than doubling to Rs 1,289.70 crore in the fourth quarter of the last fiscal year, driven by robust demand. For the full fiscal year, the company achieved a record Rs 2,421.47 crore in sales bookings, a significant jump from the previous year.

news.title: ED raids seven premises linked to Kolkata-based Merlin Group
news.para: The Enforcement Directorate has raided the properties linked to the Merlin Group in Kolkata and its leaders, intensifying its probe into a money laundering case. The agency has alleged that false land titles were created using forged paperwork, prompting serious claims of land theft.

news.title: Haryana RERA's Gurugram bench clears all pending cases till 2024
news.para: HRERA Gurugram has made remarkable progress by clearing its backlog and resolving all complaints lodged until 2024, which included a staggering 2,100 cases. Last year, they disposed of 5,024 complaints, showcasing their commitment to prompt justice.
